county_metadata.record_losses: TWO significant losses: (1) Early court records went missing 1865 (possibly destroyed by Federal sympathizers during/after Civil War). (2) 1921 clerk's office fire destroyed additional records. Losses are significant but not total.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Duplin 1784. Pre-1784 records must be sought in Duplin County. Sampson RETAINED original Duplin deed books at formation — copies now at Duplin. Fell under Fayetteville District Superior Court (SR.398). Federal census records for 1790, 1800, 1810 are INCOMPLETE.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sampson ROD portal is excellent — all deeds scanned from 1783 to present. For pre-1784, search Duplin County. State Archives hold 162 Fibredex boxes of estate records and 32 boxes of guardian records with full indexes online as PDFs. Wake Forest Z. Smith Reynolds Library holds major Baptist church records (Great Cohara/Rowan 1790-1964, Wells Chapel 1793-1934). NCGenWeb Sampson project has free transcribed records. Heritage book by Bizzell (1984) is definitive but out of print.
county_metadata.heritage_book: The Heritage of Sampson County, North Carolina, 1784-1984 (Oscar M. Bizzell, ed., Hunter Publishing, 1984)
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: images and indexes — all deeds scanned and indexed from 1783 to present

source_profiles.source_name: Sampson County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: All deeds scanned and indexed from 1783 to present. Free online. Online vital records ordering via sampsonrod.permitium.com.
Record of Wills with Cross Index 1820-1925. Deed Records with some wills 1791-1820.

source_profiles.source_name: Sampson County Estate Records at NC State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: 162 Fibredex boxes (Adams-Zachary). 32 boxes guardian records (Adams-Wynne, 1803-1968). Full box lists and indexes available online as PDFs. Record Group C.R.087.

source_profiles.source_name: Sampson County Court of Pleas and Quarter Sessions Minutes
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Minutes 1794-1851. Also Civil Action Papers 1790-1932.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Early court records lost 1865. 1921 fire destroyed additional records. Pre-1790 court records do not survive.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catalog #2657430. Digitized.

source_profiles.repository: Z. Smith Reynolds Library, Wake Forest University
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Record book 1790-1855, minute books 1790-1964, membership rolls INCLUDING enslaved persons. Available at Wake Forest, FamilySearch, and DPLA.

Clinton Independent (1856-1858) on DigitalNC — earliest Sampson paper. Sampson Democrat (1892-1924) on DigitalNC and Chronicling America. Sampson Independent (1924+) partially on DigitalNC. OldNews.com has 4,441 scans (subscription).
Indexed and images. Statewide collection with Sampson entries.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Young county with intact records.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Richmond 1899. One of NC's youngest counties. Pre-1899 records in Richmond County. St. Andrews University (now St. Andrews University) in Laurinburg. Scottish Highland heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Scotland County ROD. For pre-1899, use Richmond County records (formed from Anson 1779). Scotland County Memorial Library has local history.

source_profiles.source_name: Scotland County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage registers from county formation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Montgomery 1841. Pre-1841 records in Montgomery County (triple-burned: 1835, 1840, 1886). Reed Gold Mine (1799) — site of first documented gold find in America — is in Stanly County area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Stanly County ROD online. For pre-1841, Montgomery County records are severely limited by fires. Adjacent county records (Anson, Cabarrus) may fill gaps. Stanly County Museum has local history.

source_profiles.source_name: Stanly County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Surry 1789. Pre-1789 records in Surry County (formed from Rowan 1771). Parent of Forsyth (1849). Hanging Rock and Sauratown Mountains. Moratock Iron Furnace operated here 1843-1861.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Stokes County ROD online. For pre-1789, use Surry County records. Stokes County Historical Society has local collections. Danbury Historical Society at Moratock Park.

source_profiles.source_name: Stokes County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1790 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Rowan 1771. Pre-1771 records in Rowan County (excellent colonial records from 1753). Parent of Stokes (1789), Yadkin (1850). Mount Airy — inspiration for Mayberry in Andy Griffith Show. Pilot Mountain landmark.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — deeds from 1771. Surry County ROD has online portal. Mount Airy Museum of Regional History has genealogical collections. Surry County Genealogical Association is active.

source_profiles.source_name: Surry County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1771 and registers from 1851.

source_profiles.source_name: Mount Airy Museum of Regional History
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Regional history museum with genealogical collections covering Surry and surrounding counties. Family files, cemetery records, and local history.
